evals.prompt: I need to build a DSPy pipeline that first classifies a support ticket, then routes it to different handlers based on the category. How do I structure this as a module?
evals.expected_output: Creates a dspy.Module subclass with classify-then-route pattern

assertions.name: subclasses_dspy_module
assertions.description: Subclasses dspy.Module with __init__ and forward methods

assertions.name: self_attribute_sub_modules
assertions.description: Declares sub-modules (Predict or ChainOfThought) as self. attributes in __init__, not local variables or plain lists

assertions.name: conditional_routing
assertions.description: Uses conditional logic in forward() to route based on classification result

assertions.name: returns_prediction
assertions.description: Returns a dspy.Prediction with structured output fields

assertions.name: appropriate_module_types
assertions.description: Uses Predict for simple tasks, ChainOfThought for tasks that benefit from reasoning

evals.prompt: I optimized my DSPy module and want to save it so I can load it in production later. How does save and load work?
evals.expected_output: Shows save/load workflow with correct API

assertions.name: correct_load_workflow
assertions.description: Shows loading into a fresh instance: program = MyProgram(); program.load('path.json')

assertions.name: lm_config_not_saved
assertions.description: Mentions that dspy.configure() must be called before loading because LM config is not saved

assertions.name: save_load_scope
assertions.description: Explains what gets saved (few-shot demos, instructions) vs what does not (forward logic, model weights)

evals.prompt: How do I compose multiple DSPy modules together? I want a summarizer module that I can reuse inside a larger analysis module.
evals.expected_output: Shows nested module composition with optimizer discovery

assertions.name: custom_module_subclass
assertions.description: Creates a custom Module subclass for the summarizer

assertions.name: nested_as_self_attribute
assertions.description: Uses the summarizer as a self. attribute inside the larger module, not instantiated inside forward()

assertions.name: optimizer_traversal_explained
assertions.description: Explains that optimizers traverse the full module tree and optimize nested sub-modules automatically

assertions.name: print_for_inspection
assertions.description: Shows print(module) or named_predictors() to verify the module hierarchy
